steps.title: Recall what the y-intercept is
steps.content:
The $y$-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the $y$-axis, i.e., where $x = 0$. Substitute $x = 0$ into the equation:
$$y = \frac{45}{0 - 5} = \frac{45}{-5} = -9$$
So the $y$-intercept is $(0, -9)$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $(0, -9)$: Setting $x = 0$ gives $y = 45/(-5) = -9$. ✓
  • Choice B — $(0, 0)$: Would require $45/(0-5) = 0$, but $45/(-5) = -9 \ne 0$. ✗
  • Choice C — $(-5, 0)$: This confuses the $x$-intercept with the $y$-intercept, and even as an $x$-intercept it is wrong because setting $y = 0$ gives $45/(x-5) = 0$, which has no solution (a fraction equals zero only if its numerator is zero, but 45 is never zero). ✗
  • Choice D — $(45, 0)$: Plugging in the numerator as an intercept is not a valid method. The $y$-intercept requires $x = 0$, not $x = 45$. ✗
That is the correct answer.
